condition_precedents: Protocol complexity and amendment frequency reviewed
Site selection model updated for enrolment velocity
CRO performance-based contract negotiated (milestone / enrolment basis)
industry_sources: DSIR — R&D Statistics and Indicators 2023-24 (dsir.gov.in/reports.htm) — India clinical trial landscape and CRO adoption
OPPI — Indian Pharmaceutical Industry Report 2024 (oppiin.org) — clinical trial cost benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit active clinical trial portfolio: assess protocol complexity, site enrolment rate, CRO performance, and cost per patient enrolled vs benchmark
Redesign protocol for top-3 delayed studies: simplify endpoints, adopt decentralised trial (DCT) elements (eConsent, remote monitoring), and use risk-based monitoring
Renegotiate CRO contracts: shift from FTE-based to milestone-based pricing; consolidate to preferred CRO panel (2–3 vendors)
Track cost per patient enrolled, enrolment velocity, and protocol deviation rate by study monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per patient enrolled >30% above CRO industry benchmark for comparable therapeutic area and Phase
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Protocol simplification and CRO renegotiation will reduce per-patient cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average patient enrolment rate <80% of plan, extending study timelines and increasing fixed trial costs
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Site optimisation and DCT elements will improve enrolment velocity

diagnostic_signals.signal: CRO panel >5 vendors with no preferred vendor agreement or performance benchmarking
diagnostic_signals.confirms: CRO consolidation will improve pricing leverage and management efficiency

required_data_fields: Active clinical trial list with status, cost to date, and estimated cost to complete
Cost per patient enrolled vs industry benchmark by therapeutic area
CRO performance data (enrolment rate, protocol deviations, budget adherence)
CTMS data: site performance, screen fail rates, and enrolment milestones
DCT technology readiness assessment

condition_precedents: API and excipient should-cost models completed
Alternate source qualification (ASQ) approved by QA
Batch size / yield optimisation validated by manufacturing
industry_sources: DPCO — Drug Price Control Order and NLEM (pharmaceuticals.gov.in/national-pharmaceutical-pricing-authority) — manufacturing cost benchmarks for regulated pricing
McKinsey & Company — Productivity in Pharma Manufacturing (mckinsey.com/industries/life-sciences) — COGS reduction levers 8-18%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Decompose COGS by component: API, excipients, packaging, manufacturing overhead, and quality cost; compare vs comparable product benchmark
Conduct yield analysis by manufacturing step: identify yield losses, batch failure rate, and OOS events for root-cause and correction
Qualify alternate API/excipient suppliers under existing ANDA/NDA filings where permitted; negotiate on volume-based pricing
Track COGS per unit, batch failure rate, and API price variance monthly; rebaseline after each yield improvement project

diagnostic_signals.signal: Manufacturing COGS as % of net sales >45% for branded formulations or >35% for generics vs peer benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Yield improvement and material cost reduction will improve gross margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: Batch failure or out-of-specification rate >2% for key products generating significant reprocessing and write-off cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process optimisation will reduce failure cost and improve OEE

diagnostic_signals.signal: Single API supplier dependency for >50% of product portfolio creating price and supply vulnerability
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Supplier diversification will reduce risk and restore competitive tension

required_data_fields: COGS breakdown by product and component (API, excipients, packaging, overhead)
Batch yield data by product and manufacturing site (12 months)
API price trends and supplier concentration data
ANDA/NDA site and supplier qualification status
Regulatory filing requirements for alternate supplier qualification

condition_precedents: Physician call frequency analysis and prescriber segmentation completed
Territory redesign approved by commercial leadership
HR / severance plan for displaced reps prepared
industry_sources: OPPI — Indian Pharmaceutical Industry Report 2024 (oppiin.org) — field force productivity benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse prescriber data (IMS/IQVIA) by territory: map TRx volume, rep call frequency, and call coverage ratio against yield per call
Build territory productivity model: rank territories by revenue opportunity vs current rep cost; identify over/under-covered territories
Resize field force: consolidate under-productive territories, reassign or exit under-performing reps, redeploy to high-growth geographies
Track rep productivity (TRx growth, net sales per rep), coverage index, and SFA compliance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Rep productivity (net sales per rep) >20% below peer benchmark for comparable therapeutic area and market maturity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rightsizing and redeployment will improve revenue per rep without losing market share

diagnostic_signals.signal: Territory coverage index >120% for mature product with flat TRx trajectory (over-covered, diminishing returns)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Territory consolidation will reduce field force cost without sales impact

diagnostic_signals.signal: Field force cost as % of net sales >20% vs specialty pharma benchmark of <15%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Salesforce optimisation will improve commercial efficiency

required_data_fields: Territory-level TRx data (IQVIA/IMS) and rep call activity (SFA reports)
Revenue per rep and per territory (12 months)
Rep cost by grade and territory (salary, travel, allowances)
Coverage index and call frequency benchmarks by specialty
Market share data by product and territory

condition_precedents: Physician digital engagement platform selected and piloted
Medical affairs approval for e-promotion content
Sales force training on digital engagement completed
industry_sources: OPPI — Digital Health and Pharma Marketing Report 2024 (oppiin.org) — e-detailing adoption benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Segment HCP universe by digital adoption and prescribing behaviour; identify candidates for digital-first (e-detailing, remote) vs in-person engagement model
Launch e-detailing platform and remote rep capability for digital-preference HCPs; develop content library for digital channels
Shift field rep call mix: reduce in-person call frequency for digital-responsive HCPs; reallocate rep time to high-value non-digital prescribers
Track digital engagement metrics (e-detail views, content consumption), TRx impact by engagement channel, and rep cost-per-call monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: In-person call frequency >8/month for HCPs with confirmed digital adoption and high e-detail engagement
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digital channel shift will maintain TRx with lower cost per interaction

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per in-person detail call >₹800 vs e-detailing cost <₹200 for comparable reach and message delivery
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digital model will reduce cost per interaction materially

diagnostic_signals.signal: HCP digital adoption >40% in target specialty (measured by telemedicine usage, conference attendance online, and social media activity)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Sufficient HCP digital readiness to support engagement model shift

required_data_fields: HCP segmentation: digital adoption score, prescribing value, and current engagement frequency
E-detailing platform capability and content delivery metrics
Cost per interaction by channel (in-person vs digital vs virtual)
TRx correlation data by engagement channel for comparable HCP segments
Rep utilisation and call frequency data from SFA system

condition_precedents: Payer mix and rebate liability analysis completed per tender/formulary
Contract management system updated with all rebate obligations
Value-based evidence package developed for key formulary negotiations
industry_sources: NPPA — Price Monitoring and Resource Unit Data 2024 (pharmaceuticals.gov.in) — gross-to-net deduction benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map formulary and tender position by state, insurer, and hospital segment; identify coverage gaps vs competitor access and net price impact of rebates
Redesign rebate model: shift from volume-based to outcomes/adherence-based rebates where payers will support; model net realisation impact
Implement contract management system for rebate tracking; automate rebate accrual calculation in ERP to eliminate manual reconciliation
Track gross-to-net waterfall by product, net realisation per unit, and formulary access score quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Gross-to-net discount >30% for hospital segment, driven primarily by volume rebates with weak clinical outcomes linkage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rebate redesign to outcomes basis will improve net realisation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Rebate accrual error or reversal events >3/quarter due to manual tracking without contract management system
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will reduce accrual errors and improve P&L accuracy

diagnostic_signals.signal: Formulary access gap in key state formularies or hospital tenders vs leading competitor
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Market access strategy adjustment will improve revenue opportunity

required_data_fields: Formulary and tender status by state, insurer, and hospital segment
Gross-to-net waterfall by product and channel
Rebate contract terms and accrual data by customer
Contract management system capability assessment
Competitor pricing and formulary access data

condition_precedents: Application landscape review and redundancy map completed
Data migration and validation plan approved by IT and compliance
GxP validation completed for decommissioned systems
industry_sources: Gartner — Life Sciences IT Spending Benchmarks 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget) — pharma IT platform consolidation savings
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Inventory all commercial and clinical technology platforms (Veeva CRM, CTMS, QMS, PVG, LIMS, ERP); map to vendor, annual cost, and integration dependencies
Identify rationalization opportunities: functional duplicates, underutilised licences, and platforms replaceable by existing modules in current stack
Execute consolidation: migrate to single-vendor Veeva platform (CRM + Vault QMS + eTMF + PromoMats) where feasible; negotiate enterprise agreement
Decommission retired systems; track licence spend per user and system count monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Technology cost per employee in commercial or R&D function >₹2 L/year with >30% identified as duplicated capability
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will reduce per-head technology cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Veeva CRM deployed but Veeva Vault for QMS or eTMF not adopted, despite functionality overlap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Expanding single-vendor platform will eliminate point-solution cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Data integration costs high due to multiple system-to-system feeds between clinical and commercial platforms
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Platform consolidation will reduce integration maintenance cost

required_data_fields: Technology platform inventory with vendor, users, cost, and contract term
Functional overlap assessment across current platforms
Veeva or alternative enterprise platform capability map
Integration architecture and cost for current system-to-system feeds
Regulatory validation requirements (21 CFR Part 11, GAMP5) for platform changes

condition_precedents: Expiry monitoring system deployed (real-time shelf-life tracking)
Demand-driven production scheduling implemented (MTO for slow movers)
Returns management and donations policy updated
industry_sources: CRISIL — Pharma Supply Chain Report 2024 (crisil.com) — inventory write-off rates for Indian pharma companies
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build expiry risk dashboard: SKU-level inventory by batch, shelf-life remaining, and monthly consumption rate; flag high-risk batches (>30% risk of expiry)
Segment high-risk inventory: dose-form suitability for shelf-life extension, reprocessing under QA sign-off, or liquidation to institutional buyer at discount
Implement demand-driven production planning: set maximum production run length for slow-moving SKUs; reduce build-to-stock for short shelf-life products
Track write-off rate (as % of procurement value), average remaining shelf life at shipment, and expiry waste monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Expiry write-off >1.5% of total COGS annually, indicating structural overproduction or demand planning inaccuracy
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand-led production planning and FEFO enforcement will reduce expiry risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Inventory days-on-hand >120 days for products with ≤24-month shelf life
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Excess inventory with limited shelf life creates material expiry risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Short-dated inventory (≤6 months shelf life) representing >10% of current stock value
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Proactive liquidation or shelf-life extension will recover value

required_data_fields: Batch-level inventory data: SKU, quantity, manufacture date, and expiry date
Monthly consumption data by SKU (12 months)
Historical write-off data by SKU and reason code
Production schedule and build-to-stock vs make-to-order split
Shelf-life extension regulatory feasibility by product

condition_precedents: Common technical document (CTD) templates standardised
Regulatory information management system (RIMS) deployed
Rolling submission strategy agreed with regulatory bodies where applicable
industry_sources: CDSCO — Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation Annual Report 2023-24 (cdsco.gov.in) — regulatory submission timelines India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Regulatory Affairs Efficiency: validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times

condition_precedents: Adverse event case processing volume benchmarked (> 5,000 cases/year for viability)
AI-assisted intake and triage tool validated for regulatory compliance
Regulatory authority acceptance confirmed for AI-aided ICSR processing
industry_sources: CDSCO — Pharmacovigilance Programme of India (pvpi.gov.in) — PV reporting requirements and automation opportunities
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit ICSR (Individual Case Safety Report) processing workflow: measure intake volume by source, processing time per case, and FTE-hours by activity
Implement E2B gateway for electronic exchange with regulators (CDSCO, EMA, FDA); automate intake from digital channels (e-commerce, social media listening)
Deploy AI-assisted medical coding (MedDRA) and narrative writing tool; run parallel with manual processing for 60 days before go-live
Track ICSR processing time, coding accuracy, and on-time submission rate to CDSCO monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: ICSR processing volume growing >20% YoY with manual processing model causing backlog and on-time submission risk
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will absorb volume growth without proportionate FTE increase

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average ICSR processing time >3 days per case for non-serious or >8 days for serious reports vs automated benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI-assisted processing will reduce cycle time and regulatory risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: No E2B electronic gateway with CDSCO requiring manual PDF submission of safety reports
diagnostic_signals.confirms: E2B gateway will eliminate manual submission and reduce error risk

required_data_fields: ICSR volume by source (HCP, patient, literature, post-market surveillance) and month (12 months)
Processing time and FTE-hours per case by activity (intake, coding, medical review, submission)
CDSCO and ICH E2B compliance requirements for electronic submission
PV database (Argus/ArisG) and E2B gateway capability assessment
AI medical coding tool options and validation requirements

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
